title: Explain Azure Blob Storage vs Azure SQL Database
description: Explain when to store data in Azure Blob Storage versus Azure SQL Database.
prompt: Compare Azure Blob Storage and Azure SQL Database for an app storing uploaded documents and user records.

examples.input: Data: PDFs, profile rows, status fields
examples.output: Use Blob Storage for files and SQL for relational records.

strong_answer_includes: Blob Storage is for unstructured objects like files, images, logs, and backups
Azure SQL is for relational tables, queries, joins, and transactions
A common design stores files in Blob Storage and metadata in SQL
Mentions cost, access pattern, or consistency needs
common_gaps: Treats Blob Storage like a relational database

sample_answer: Blob Storage is best for unstructured objects such as PDFs, images, exports, and raw log files. Azure SQL Database is best for structured relational data such as users, permissions, document metadata, and workflow status because it supports SQL queries, constraints, and transactions. For a document app, I would store the file itself in Blob Storage and keep the file URL, owner, status, and audit fields in SQL. That keeps file storage cost-effective while preserving queryable business data.
canonical_solution_summary: Blob for objects; SQL for relational data and metadata.
